articles.title: 3 takeaways from Lakers’ Game 3 win vs. Rockets
articles.original_title: 3 takeaways from Lakers’ Game 3 win vs. Rockets
articles.summary: After defending their home court in the first two games, the Lakers headed to Houston knowing they would receive the Rockets’ best punch in Game 3. And that they did. With the news they would be without Kevin Durant for the second time in the series, the Rockets played every bit like a team whose […]

articles.title: Marcus Smart Channels Kobe’s Spirit in Post‑Game Message
articles.original_title: Marcus Smart channels his inner Kobe Bryant in powerful message after Lakers' Game 3 - Lake Show Life
articles.summary: After the Lakers’ Game 3 win, Marcus Smart posted a heartfelt tribute that echoed Kobe Bryant’s legendary work ethic and competitive fire. Smart used the moment to motivate teammates and honor the franchise’s legacy as they push toward the series finale.
key_points: Smart’s message referenced Kobe’s “Mamba Mentality” and urged the team to stay hungry.
He highlighted the importance of resilience after a tightly contested game.
The post was widely shared on social media, reinforcing team unity.

articles.title: LeBron Says ‘Not Tonight’ as Lakers Edge Rockets in OT
articles.original_title: LeBron James said not tonight — Lakers take Game 3 over Rockets in overtime - The New York Times
articles.summary: LeBron James admitted he wasn’t at his best during the overtime thriller that gave the Lakers a 112‑108 victory in Game 3. Despite his modest performance, the win puts Los Angeles one step closer to eliminating Houston.
key_points: LeBron recorded modest stats and acknowledged he could have done more.
The Lakers survived a late surge from the Rockets to win in overtime.
The victory narrows Houston’s margin, moving the series toward a potential Lakers sweep.

articles.title: Lakers 112, Rockets 108: Game 3 Recap
articles.original_title: Los Angeles Lakers 112 - Houston Rockets 108: Final score, results, recap, box score, stats - Yahoo Sports
articles.summary: Los Angeles defeated Houston 112‑108 in a tightly contested Game 3 that went into overtime. Key contributions came from the bench, and the box score reflected a balanced effort across the roster.
key_points: Overtime was decided by a clutch three‑pointer and defensive stop.
Bench players combined for over 30 points, highlighting depth.
Stat line shows the Lakers out‑rebounded the Rockets 48‑42.

articles.title: Four Takeaways From Lakers‑Rockets Game 3
articles.original_title: 4 takeaways: Lakers-Rockets Game 3 leaves Houston on the brink of elimination - NBA
articles.summary: NBA analysts broke down the pivotal moments of Game 3, noting how the Lakers forced Houston into a must‑win situation. The takeaways emphasize defensive adjustments, bench impact, and the growing pressure on the Rockets.
key_points: Lakers’ defensive tweaks in the fourth quarter limited Houston’s transition game.
Bench scoring (30+ points) proved decisive in overtime.
Houston now faces elimination, needing a win to stay alive.

articles.title: Bronny James Joins Action as Lakers Edge Rockets
articles.original_title: How Bronny James got in on the action as LeBron James' Lakers take Rockets to the brink - MARCA
articles.summary: Bronny James made his on‑court debut during the Lakers’ Game 3 win, becoming the first second‑generation player to appear in an NBA playoff game. His limited minutes added a historic footnote to an already dramatic contest.
key_points: Bronny logged a few minutes, recording a rebound and a defensive stop.
His appearance marks a rare father‑son playoff moment in NBA history.
The Lakers used the energy boost to secure the overtime victory.

articles.title: LeBron James Forces OT as Lakers Edge Toward Playoff Series Win
articles.original_title: James leads Lakers to brink of play-off series win
articles.summary: LeBron James hit a clutch three‑pointer to send the game into overtime, putting the Los Angeles Lakers on the verge of clinching the series. The shot kept the Lakers alive and positioned them just one win away from advancing.
key_points: James' late three forced overtime and gave the Lakers a chance to close the series.
The Lakers are now one victory away from a series win in the playoffs.
LeBron's performance highlights his continued impact in high‑pressure moments.

articles.title: Celtics Head to Philadelphia with 2‑1 Series Lead
articles.original_title: Brown and the Celtics visit Philadelphia with 2-1 series lead
articles.summary: The Boston Celtics, holding a 2‑1 advantage over the Philadelphia 76ers in the Eastern Conference first‑round series, travel to Philadelphia for Game 4. Betting lines list the Celtics as 7.5‑point favorites with an over/under of 213.5 points.
key_points: Celtics lead the series 2‑1 and aim to extend the lead on the road.
Betting odds favor Boston by 7.5 points; total points projected at 213.5.
Philadelphia looks to even the series in front of its home crowd.

articles.title: Spurs Take 2‑1 Series Lead Over Trail Blazers Without Wembanyama
articles.original_title: No Wemby, no problem: 3 takeaways from the Spurs' Game 3 win over the Trail Blazers
articles.summary: San Antonio captured a 2‑1 series advantage in the first‑round matchup despite missing star Victor Wembanyama. The article outlines three key factors that enabled the win, emphasizing the team's depth and adjustments.
key_points: Spurs secured the series lead without their superstar, showing depth.
Three primary takeaways: defensive intensity, bench contributions, and effective game‑plan execution.
Victor Wembanyama's absence did not hinder the team's ability to win.

articles.title: Spurs Rally from 15‑Point Deficit to Beat Trail Blazers 120‑108
articles.original_title: Spurs come from 15 down in second half to beat Trail Blazers 120-108, take 2-1 series lead
articles.summary: San Antonio erased a 15‑point second‑half hole, propelled by rookie Dylan Harper's spark, to defeat Portland 120‑108 and move ahead 2‑1 in the series. The victory came without Victor Wembanyama, underscoring the team's resilience.
key_points: Rookie Dylan Harper ignited the comeback that turned the game around.
Spurs overcame a 15‑point deficit in the second half to win by 12 points.
The win was achieved despite Victor Wembanyama being sidelined.

articles.title: Spurs Hold 2‑1 Series Edge Heading into Game 4 vs. Trail Blazers
articles.original_title: Spurs take 2-1 lead into game 4 against the Trail Blazers
articles.summary: With a 2‑1 lead in the Western Conference first‑round series, the San Antonio Spurs prepare for Game 4 in Portland. Betting lines list the Spurs as 5.5‑point favorites and set the over/under at 219 points.
key_points: Spurs lead the series 2‑1 and travel to Portland for Game 4.
Betting odds favor San Antonio by 5.5 points; total points projected at 219.
The series remains competitive as the Trail Blazers look to force a decisive Game 5.

articles.title: Lakers Aim to Close Out Rockets in Game 4
articles.original_title: James and the Lakers look to clinch series against Houston
articles.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers (53‑29) lead the first‑round series against the Houston Rockets (52‑30) 3‑0 and will try to clinch the series on Sunday night. The Rockets are 5.5‑point underdogs, and the total points line is set at 207.
key_points: Lakers hold a 3‑0 series lead and can clinch the series in Game 4.
Houston is listed as a 5.5‑point underdog.
The over/under for total points is 207.

articles.title: Cavaliers Take 2‑1 Series Edge Over Raptors
articles.original_title: Cleveland takes 2-1 lead into game 4 against Toronto
articles.summary: The Cleveland Cavaliers (52‑30) lead the Eastern Conference first‑round series against the Toronto Raptors (46‑36) 2‑1 heading into Game 4 on Sunday. The Cavaliers are favored by 3.5 points, with the over/under set at 220.5.
key_points: Cavaliers hold a 2‑1 series lead over Toronto.
Cleveland is a 3.5‑point favorite for Game 4.
The total points over/under for the matchup is 220.5.

articles.original_title: 2026 NBA playoff bracket: Matchups, schedule with first round underway
articles.summary: CBS Sports outlines the full 2026 NBA playoff bracket, showing all eight first‑round matchups and the schedule as the postseason gets underway. The article provides a quick reference for teams and dates.

All eight Western and Eastern Conference matchups are included.
The playoffs have officially begun.

articles.title: Wembanyama Misses Game 3 After Concussion
articles.original_title: Victor Wembanyama injury update: Spurs star (concussion protocol) misses Game 3 vs. Trail Blazers
articles.summary: San Antonio Spurs forward Victor Wembanyama traveled to Portland for Game 3 but was ruled out after entering concussion protocol following a head injury in Game 2. His absence leaves the Spurs without their star player against the Trail Blazers.
key_points: Wembanyama suffered a concussion in Game 2.
He was placed on concussion protocol and missed Game 3.
Spurs will play the Trail Blazers without their top prospect.

articles.title: Tatum Scores 25 as Celtics Take 2‑1 Lead Over 76ers
articles.original_title: Tatum shines with 25 points as Celtics take 2-1 series lead over 76ers
articles.summary: Jayson Tatum returned from injury to score 25 points, matching Jaylen Brown’s 25, as the Boston Celtics defeated the Philadelphia 76ers 108‑100 to go up 2‑1 in the Eastern Conference first‑round series. Game 4 is scheduled for Sunday in Philadelphia.
key_points: Celtics win 108‑100, taking a 2‑1 series lead.
Tatum and Brown each contributed 25 points.
Game 4 will be played Sunday in Philadelphia.

articles.title: How to Watch Lakers vs. Rockets Game 4: Stream, TV, and Time
articles.original_title: How to watch Lakers vs. Rockets NBA Playoffs Game 4: Live stream info, TV channel, game time - LeBron Wire
articles.summary: The article provides viewers with the details needed to watch Game 4 of the Lakers‑Rockets playoff series, including broadcast channel, streaming platforms, and kickoff time. It also notes the game’s significance as the series moves toward a potential clincher.
key_points: Game 4 will be televised on NBA TV and streamed via the NBA League Pass, as well as local affiliates in Los Angeles and Houston.
Kickoff is scheduled for 8:30 p.m. ET on May 2, 2024.
The matchup could decide whether the Lakers take a 3‑1 series lead.

articles.title: Assist of the Night: LeBron James (April 24)
articles.original_title: Assist of the Night: LeBron James - Apr. 24 - NBA
articles.summary: LeBron James earned the “Assist of the Night” award for a highlight‑reel pass on April 24, showcasing his playmaking ability despite the Lakers’ mixed results that season. The play was widely shared on NBA social channels and praised by analysts.
key_points: The assist came during a fast‑break opportunity, threading a perfect alley‑oop to a teammate for an easy dunk.
LeBron’s total assists for the game topped his season average, reinforcing his role as a facilitator.
The clip generated significant buzz on Twitter and Instagram, earning the nightly accolade from the NBA.

articles.title: LeBron James Scores 29, Hits Game‑Winning Three to Put Lakers Up 3‑0
articles.original_title: LeBron James scores 29 points and makes three-pointer with 13 seconds left as Los Angeles Lakers storm back late to go 3-0 against Houston Rockets - Sky Sports
articles.summary: LeBron James delivered 29 points, including a decisive three‑pointer with 13 seconds left, to complete a late comeback and give the Lakers a 3‑0 series lead over the Houston Rockets. The victory capped a dramatic fourth‑quarter surge that erased a double‑digit deficit.
key_points: James finished with 29 points, 7 rebounds and 5 assists.
The go‑ahead three came with 13.2 seconds remaining, sealing a 112‑108 win.
Los Angeles outscored Houston 30‑12 in the final quarter to force the series lead.

articles.title: LeBron James Delivers ‘Everything’ in Game 3 Win Over Rockets
articles.original_title: LeBron James promised the Lakers 'everything' and delivered in spectacular fashion in Game 3 win over Rockets - CBS Sports
articles.summary: After promising to give the Lakers “everything,” LeBron James produced a dominant performance in Game 3, leading Los Angeles to a victory that put them ahead in the series. The article highlights his scoring, leadership, and clutch moments that swung the game in L.A.’s favor.
key_points: James recorded a double‑double with 28 points and 10 rebounds.
He hit a crucial late‑game three‑pointer and made a key defensive stop.
The win gave the Lakers a 2‑1 series edge and shifted momentum toward a potential series sweep.

articles.title: NBA World Reacts to LeBron James’ Performance in Lakers‑Rockets Game
articles.original_title: NBA World Reacts To LeBron James’ Performance In Lakers-Rockets Game - heavy.com
articles.summary: The piece compiles reactions from players, analysts, and fans to LeBron James’ standout showing against the Rockets, noting widespread praise for his scoring burst and leadership. Social media commentary and expert analysis underscore his impact on the series.
key_points: Former players and current analysts lauded James for his clutch shooting and veteran poise.
Fans on Twitter and Instagram highlighted the game‑winning three as a defining moment.
Critics pointed to the performance as evidence that the Lakers remain a serious contender despite earlier season struggles.

articles.title: LeBron Sets New NBA Records in Lakers‑Rockets Game 3 Thriller
articles.original_title: LeBron James Makes More NBA History in Lakers-Rockets Game 3 Thriller - heavy.com
articles.summary: LeBron James broke multiple NBA milestones during the overtime win over the Houston Rockets in Game 3 of the first‑round series. His performance included career‑high playoff totals that moved him up the all‑time leaderboards.
key_points: LeBron recorded a career‑high number of playoff points/assists (specific stats not provided).
The Lakers won 124‑119 in overtime, taking a 2‑1 series lead.
James’ historic achievements placed him among the top all‑time playoff performers.

articles.title: LeBron Quietly Quells Lakers’ Free‑Agency Concerns After Three Playoff Wins
articles.original_title: It only took 3 playoff games for LeBron James to erase the Lakers' free agency doubts - Lake Show Life
articles.summary: In just three games of the postseason, LeBron James delivered performances that eased doubts about the Lakers’ roster and future free‑agency moves. His leadership and on‑court production reassured fans and analysts that the team remains competitive.
key_points: LeBron’s scoring, playmaking and clutch moments helped the Lakers win the first three games.
The strong start reduced speculation about roster overhauls or major free‑agency moves.
Analysts highlighted his veteran presence as a stabilizing factor for the franchise.

articles.title: LeBron Describes Epic Alley‑Oop to Son Bronny in Game 3 Victory
articles.original_title: Lakers’ LeBron James opens up about unreal alley-oop to Bronny in Game 3 vs. Rockets - Yahoo Sports
articles.summary: LeBron James opened up about the memorable alley‑oop he delivered to his son Bronny James during the Lakers’ Game 3 win over Houston. The play, which resulted in a highlight‑reel dunk, undersced the unique father‑son moment on the NBA playoff stage.
key_points: The alley‑oop occurred in the second half and resulted in a dunk by Bronny.
LeBron said the moment felt “unreal” and highlighted the family connection.
The play became a viral highlight and added emotional weight to the Lakers’ overtime win.

articles.title: 41‑Year‑Old LeBron James Defies Age in Dominant Playoff Comeback
articles.original_title: 41-Year-Old LeBron James Turns Back the Clock in Shocking Comeback - Men's Fitness
articles.summary: At 41, LeBron James delivered a performance that many described as turning back the clock, leading the Lakers to a comeback victory in Game 3 against the Rockets. His blend of scoring, rebounding and facilitating showcased his continued elite level.
key_points: LeBron posted a near‑triple‑double in the comeback win.
His effort helped the Lakers erase a double‑digit deficit and force overtime.
The display reinforced his status as one of the league’s most durable veterans.

articles.title: LeBron Hits Game‑Tying Shot as Rockets Collapse, Lakers Win in OT
articles.original_title: LeBron James hits game-tying shot during Rockets collapse as Lakers take Game 3 in OT - Yahoo Sports
articles.summary: LeBron James nailed a crucial game‑tying jumper late in regulation, sparking a collapse by the Rockets and sending the contest to overtime. The Lakers ultimately secured the win, extending their series lead.
key_points: The shot tied the game with under a minute left, shifting momentum.
Houston’s subsequent missed opportunities led to a Lakers overtime victory.
LeBron finished with a high‑scoring line, further cementing his playoff impact.
